Old-reviewer reuse and self-review remain prohibited.

Archived and closed all persisted child-agent sessions; interrupted the stalled round-24 reviewer after bounded waits and attempted both direct replacement and nested fresh-default relay allocation.

Ran the full docs/schema/inventory/link/archive/handoff proof wall and staged GitNexus low-risk change detection, then committed the review-pending checkpoint without promoting completion or any runtime gate.

decisions.summary: Keep HCM-0.4 documentation/design-only; no Rust, Cargo, CLI, Tauri, or Substrate implementation is authorized.
Use one typed SDK operation/DTO/schema boundary with thin JSON, Tauri, and transitional process adapters.
decisions.authority_effect: freezes target ownership only; runtime gates remain open

decisions.summary: Keep the process bridge transitional and crates.io Rust consumption permanent, with separate proof and deletion gates.
Do not declare the slice complete without a different fresh clean reviewer over the exact current subject.

Treat private bridge-control state as non-authoritative protocol metadata with exact framing, identity, crash recovery, and persistent terminal-memory semantics; keep all runtime gates open until implementation proof.
decisions.authority_effect: documents the pending target contract only

Fresh review round 16 identified an actionable committed-result identity or commit-uncertain bridge-recovery gap. The parent applied a candidate remediation, but the required different-fresh re-review could not be allocated.

Fresh review round 16 identified an actionable committed-result identity or commit-uncertain bridge-recovery gap. The parent applied a candidate remediation, but the required different-fresh re-review could not be allocated.
Retained-result compaction could strand delayed exact replay during commit-uncertain bridge recovery. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
Release-to-absence could not make lost release acknowledgments idempotent and fail closed. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
A released hold ID could ambiguously reopen. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
The complete request/open recovery identity was not required durable before open transmission. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
The bridge ordering text required release acknowledgment before open and therefore deadlocked recovery. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
The exact terminal release frame/evidence was not required durable before first release transmission. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
The recovery frames had no legal private binary entry point. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
Private-control fingerprints lacked exact recomputable closures. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
Malformed control-frame admission and state precedence were incomplete. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
Released-tombstone aggregate storage was falsely described as bounded. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
The open frame lacked the schema-bound preimage needed to recompute nested identities. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
Release identity did not cryptographically bind the exact open identity. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
Proven-noncommit evidence could not truthfully represent child-not-spawned outcomes. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.
The control acknowledgment fields were not deterministic for every state/event cell. The parent remediated it before the next different-fresh review attempt.

blockers.resume_condition: Resume in a collaboration runtime with a reset live agent registry or an exposed built-in close capability, then execute the exact round-24 dispatch before any completion claim.

resume.objective: Resume HCM-0.4 at the mandatory fresh round-24 review wall; do not start another slice.
resume.condition: The live built-in agent registry is reset or exposes working close semantics so a new isolated default reviewer can be allocated.
allowed_scope: Execute the exact round-24 dispatch with a new isolated built-in default read-only reviewer.
If findings remain, remediate only HCM-0.4 and dispatch a different fresh reviewer.
If clean, rerun the full proof wall, create a reviewed primary completion commit, and write a completed parent handoff/ledger closeout.
prohibited_scope: Do not reuse any prior HCM-0.4 reviewer or self-approve.
Do not implement Rust or modify Cargo, CLI, Tauri, or Substrate runtime code.
Do not start HCM-0.5, HCM-0.6, or another slice automatically.
Do not promote SDK/JSON/Tauri/Substrate/publication runtime gates without real-path proof.
acceptance_criteria: The exact round-24 subject manifest replays from the review-pending checkpoint.
A new isolated default reviewer returns clean, or every new finding is remediated and re-reviewed by another different fresh reviewer.
The full proof wall and staged GitNexus change detection pass.
A completed v1.2 parent handoff binds the final clean review and reviewed primary commit.
stop_conditions: If fresh built-in delegation remains unavailable after a reset context, record capability unavailability rather than reusing a reviewer or self-approving.
Stop on scope expansion beyond HCM-0.4 or any request to implement Rust.
